zh.Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire,: 英國史洛普爾艾頓 武朋 馮 維亞爾德 波斯
zh.Telford, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾特爾福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margery de Eyton於1446年出生在英國史洛普爾特爾福德時，她的父親Sir Nicholas de Eyton49歲，母親Katherine Talbot28歲。她和Sir John William Richards至少有2個兒子。她去世於1510年在英國薩默塞特阿勒享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margery de Eyton于1446年出生在英国史洛普爾特爾福德时，她的父亲Sir Nicholas de Eyton49岁，母亲Katherine Talbot28岁。她和Sir John William Richards至少有2个儿子。她去世于1510年在英国薩默塞特阿勒享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Eyton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Eyton (Shrops) or Eaton (Cheshire, Leics, Staffs), or Eaton Bray (Beds), Cold Eaton or Long Eaton (Derbys).

variants.name: Ayton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Ayton (North Yorkshire) or Ayton (Berwickshire). The former is derived from Old Norse á ‘river, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead’. The later takes its name from the River Eye (likely from Old English ēa ‘river, stream’) + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margery de Eyton was born in 1446, in Telford, Shropshire, England, her father, Sir Nicholas de Eyton, was 49 and her mother, Katherine Talbot, was 28. She had at least 2 sons with Sir John William Richards. She died in 1510, in Aller, Somerset, England, at the age of 64.

fr.Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire,: Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margery d’Eyton est née en 1446, à Telford, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas d’Eyton, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Katherine Talbot, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir John William Richards. Elle est décédée en 1510, à Aller, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margery de Eyton 1446 in Telford, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Nicholas de Eyton 49 und ihre Mutter Katherine Talbot 28. Sie hatte mit Sir John William Richards mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1510 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Aller, Somerset, England.

ko.Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire,: 영국 슈럽실르 이턴 업 테 위드 무르스
ko.lifeSummary: Margery de Eyton(이)가 1446년 영국 슈럽실르 텔퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Nicholas de Eyton(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Talbot(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Sir John William Richards(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 서머세트 앨러에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire,: эитон упон т вилд мурс, шропшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margery de Eyton родилась в 1446 г. в местности телфорд, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Nicholas de Eyton было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Talbot, 28. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Sir John William Richards. Она умерла в 1510 г. в местности аллир, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 64 лет.
